chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Inheritance of Two Children Who Are Married, and Then One of Them Dies

text: 5663 - رَوَى اَلنَّضْرُ بْنُ سُوَيْدٍ عَنِ اَلْقَاسِمِ بْنِ سُلَيْمَانَ عَنْ عُبَيْدِ بْنِ زُرَارَةَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : أَنَّهُ سَأَلَهُ عَنِ اَلصَّبِيِّ يُزَوَّجُ اَلصَّبِيَّةَ هَلْ يَتَوَارَثَانِ فَقَالَ "إِذَا كَانَ أَبَوَاهُمَا اَللَّذَانِ زَوَّجَاهُمَا فَنَعَمْ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5663 - Al-Nadr ibn Suwayd narrated from Al-Qasim ibn Sulayman, from Ubayd ibn Zurarah, who said: He asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a boy being married to a girl - do they inherit from one another?
Imam (as) said: "If it was their parents who arranged their marriage, then yes, they do inherit from each other."
